Question: How did Paul become an apostle of Christ?
Response: Paul became an apostle of Christ through the will of God.

Question: What does Paul say is his relationship with Timothy?
Response: Paul calls Timothy his “beloved child.”

Question: When Paul remembers Timothy in his prayers, what does Paul long to do?
Response: Paul longs to see Timothy.

Question: In Timothy’s family, who else had genuine faith before Timothy did?
Response: Timothy’s grandmother and mother both had genuine faith.

Question: What kind of spirit did God give Timothy?
Response: God gave Timothy a spirit of power and love and discipline.

Question: What does Paul tell Timothy not to do?
Response: Paul tells Timothy not to be ashamed of the testimony about the Lord.

Question: What does Paul tell Timothy to do?
Response: Paul tells Timothy to suffer for the gospel together with Paul.

Question: When was God’s plan and grace given to us?
Response: God’s plan and grace was given to us before eternal times.

Question: How did God reveal his plan of salvation?
Response: God’s plan of salvation was revealed through the appearing of our Savior Christ Jesus.

Question: When Jesus appeared, what did he do regarding death, life, and immortality?
Response: Jesus put an end to death, and brought to light life and immortality through the gospel.

Question: What is Paul confident that God is able to do for him which makes Paul not ashamed of the gospel?
Response: Paul is persuaded that God is able to keep the deposit that Paul has entrusted to God until that day.

Question: What is Timothy to do with the good deposit God has committed to him?
Response: Timothy is to guard through the Holy Spirit the good deposit God has committed to him.

Question: What did all of Paul’s Asian companions do to him?
Response: All those in Asia turned away from Paul.

Question: Why does Paul ask the Lord to grant mercy to the household of Onesiphorus?
Response: Paul asks the Lord to grant mercy to the household of Onesiphorus because Onesiphorus refreshed Paul and was not ashamed of Paul’s chain.

Question: What did Onesiphorus do for Paul when Paul was in Rome?
Response: Onesiphorus diligently sought Paul in Rome and found him.

Question: What does Paul ask the Lord to grant to Onesiphorus?
Response: Paul asks the Lord to grant mercy to Onesiphorus.

Question: What is able to strengthen Timothy?
Response: The grace that is in Christ Jesus can strengthen Timothy.

Question: To whom is Timothy to entrust the message Paul has taught him?
Response: Timothy is to entrust the message to faithful men who will be able to teach others also.

Question: As an illustration for Timothy, Paul says a good soldier does not entangle himself in what?
Response: A good soldier does not entangle himself in the affairs of life.

Question: As he writes to Timothy, in what condition is Paul suffering for his preaching the word of God?
Response: Paul is suffering by being chained like a criminal.

Question: What does Paul say is not bound?
Response: The word of God is not bound.

Question: Why does Paul endure all these things?
Response: Paul endures all things for those chosen by God, that they may obtain the salvation that is in Christ Jesus.

Question: What is Christ’s promise to those who endure?
Response: Those who endure will reign with Christ.

Question: What is Christ’s warning to those who deny him?
Response: Those who deny Christ, Christ will deny.

Question: About what should Timothy warn the people not to quarrel?
Response: Timothy should warn the people not to quarrel about words, which is useful for nothing.

Question: What false doctrine were the two men who wandered from the truth saying?
Response: They were saying that the resurrection had already happened.

Question: How are the believers to prepare themselves for every good work?
Response: The believers are to clean themselves from dishonorable use on order to be ready for every good work.

Question: From what is Timothy to flee?
Response: Timothy is to flee youthful lusts.

Question: What does Paul say that a servant of the Lord must be?
Response: A servant of the Lord must be patient, kind to all, and able to teach.

Question: How must a servant of the Lord treat those who oppose him?
Response: A servant of the Lord must educate in meekness those who oppose him.

Question: What has the devil done with unbelievers?
Response: The devil has trapped and captured the unbelievers for his will.

Question: What does Paul say will come in the last days?
Response: Paul says difficult times will come in the last days.

Question: In the last days, what will people love instead of God?
Response: In the last days, people will love themselves and love money instead of God.

Question: In the last days, what other thing will people love instead of God?
Response: In the last days, people will love pleasure instead of God.

Question: What does Paul tell Timothy to do with those who have only a form of godliness?
Response: Paul tells Timothy to turn away from those who have only a form of godliness.

Question: What do some of these ungodly men do?
Response: Some of these ungodly men enter households and captivate foolish women who are led away by various desires.

Question: How are these ungodly men like Jannes and Jambres in the Old Testament?
Response: These ungodly men oppose the truth just as Jannes and Jambres did to Moses.

Question: Instead of the false teachers, who has Timothy followed?
Response: Timothy has followed Paul.

Question: From what did the Lord rescue Paul?
Response: The Lord rescued Paul from all his persecutions and sufferings.

Question: What does Paul say will happen to all those who want to live in a godly manner?
Response: Paul says that all those who want to live in a godly manner will be persecuted.

Question: Who will become worse in the last days?
Response: Evil people and imposters will become worse in the last days.

Question: From what time in Timothy’s life had he known the sacred writings?
Response: Timothy knew the sacred writings from childhood.

Question: How did all the Scripture come into being?
Response: All Scripture is God-breathed.

Question: For what is all Scripture profitable?
Response: All Scripture is profitable for teaching, reproof, correction, and training in righteousness.

Question: What is the purpose of training a person in the Scriptures?
Response: A person is trained in the Scriptures so that he is proficient, equipped for every good work.

Question: Who is Jesus Christ going to judge?
Response: Jesus Christ will judge the living and the dead.

Question: What did Paul solemnly command Timothy to do?
Response: Paul solemnly commanded Timothy to preach the Word.

Question: Paul warned that the time will come when people will do what regarding doctrine?
Response: People will not endure sound teaching, but will listen to teachings that agree with their own desires.

Question: What work and ministry had Timothy been given to do?
Response: Timothy had been given the work and ministry of an evangelist.

Question: What time in his life did Paul say had now come?
Response: Paul said that his time of departure had come.

Question: What reward did Paul say all those who love Christ’s appearing will receive?
Response: Paul said that all those who love Christ’s appearing will receive the crown of righteousness.

Question: Why did Paul’s companion Demas leave him?
Response: Demas left Paul because he loved this present age.

Question: Who was the only companion of Paul to still be with him?
Response: Only Luke was still with Paul.

Question: According to what did Paul said that the Lord would repay Alexander?
Response: Paul said that the Lord will repay Alexander according to his deeds.

Question: Which people appeared with Paul at his first defense?
Response: At Paul’s first defense, no one appeared with Paul.
